name: Should I hire a lawyer for my work vehicle accident, or just deal with insurance myself?

mainEntity.name: Should I hire a lawyer for my work vehicle accident, or just deal with insurance myself?

acceptedAnswer.text: If you have significant injuries, you should consult a lawyer. Insurance companies often settle for the minimum, but an attorney helps ensure you receive full compensation for future medical needs, permanent disability, and lost wages.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Insurance adjusters often push for quick settlements before the full extent of your injuries is known. After a work vehicle accident, your 'damages' aren't just your current ER bill; they include future physical therapy, lost earning capacity, and potential permanent partial disability (PPD) ratings. Accepting a check early usually requires signing a release that ends your right to any future payments for that injury.

Common risks of handling it yourself:
- Undervaluing permanent impairments.
- Failing to account for 'subrogation' liens from your health insurance.
- Overlooking the ability to file a third-party claim against the other driver.

If your injuries require more than a single doctor's visit, the 'math' of a workers' comp claim becomes complicated. A lawyer ensures that every dollar for future care and missed work is calculated before any papers are signed. Most workers' comp attorneys work on a contingency fee, meaning they only get paid if they win more for you.
